title: Bitfarms Converts Macquarie Debt Facility to $300M Project Financing
text: Bitfarms converted previously announced private debt facility for up to $300 million from Macquarie Group's Commodities and Global Markets business to project-specific financing facility for development of data center campus in Panther Creek, Pennsylvania. Company drew additional $50 million from converted facility for total of $100 million drawn through closing to accelerate long-lead time equipment purchases and advance HPC/AI development. $300 million facility to fund Phase 1 of Panther Creek data center development with energization expected by year-end 2026 and on track to break ground on Panther Creek data center in Q4 2025. Panther Creek campus described as 350 MW HPC/AI development. Joshua Stevens noted Pennsylvania quickly emerging as new AI infrastructure hub and described Bitfarms as well-positioned to deliver state-of-the-art digital infrastructure. Conversion to project-level financing enables accelerated equipment purchases and HPC/AI infrastructure development while providing greater flexibility for construction timelines.
